title: Agregasi Ketetanggaan dan Fitur Berbasis Jalur
scope: Structure-derived features: neighborhood aggregation and path-based features.
key_concepts: Beyond single-node metrics, we can derive features by aggregating information from a node's immediate neighbors (similar to spatial lags, but on graphs).
Aggregation functions (mean, sum, max) summarize the attributes of a node's neighbors. For example, the average age of a user's friends.
Path-based features look at sequences of nodes. Shortest path length between two nodes or the number of common neighbors are powerful relational features.
This converts local graph topology into a flat, tabular format suitable for standard machine learning models.

definitions.meaning: Proses merangkum atribut-atribut dari simpul-simpul yang terhubung langsung dengan sebuah simpul target (misalnya, rata-rata atau nilai maksimum).
Fitur yang dihitung berdasarkan rute atau lompatan antar simpul, seperti jarak terpendek.

examples: Recommender systems: if predicting whether user A will buy product B, a feature could be the number of shortest paths of length 3 connecting A to B.
exclude: Complex random walk math (save for Node2Vec).
relations: Bridges the gap between basic degree features (s04) and the message-passing mechanism of GNNs (s07).
